title: Reis Upbringing Vs Kaworus
wikitext:
[]: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Thu Apr 04, 2013 4:51 pm I've always been bothered by how different the two angel vessels were. Other than looks and general detachment from reality, they have absolutely nothing in common. The only explanation for their current personalities are the environments that they grew up in, right? Rei was raised by Gendo and, though he intended from the beginning to use her as a tool and was a generally aloof father figure, he was a father figure none the less, and we see both of them at their happiest around each other. Their relationship wasn't healthy, but there was clearly love between them. Kaworu, on the other hand, we no next to nothing about. He was raised in secret in some undisclosed location and that's about it. Judging from the very brief interaction we see between him and SEELE and the fact that he seems to be well aware of his identity as an angel, we can assume he was treated as even less of a person than Rei was. So why, exactly, is Kaworu so much more extroverted and friendly than Rei is? Am I overlooking something?

[]: Re: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by Oenara » Thu Apr 04, 2013 5:07 pm Güf Trüp wrote: The only explanation for their current personalities are the environments that they grew up in, right?

[]: Re: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Apr 04, 2013 6:33 pm Güf Trüp wrote: he was a father figure none the less, and we see both of them at their happiest around each other. Their relationship wasn't healthy, but there was clearly love between them.

[]: Re: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Thu Apr 04, 2013 6:37 pm Güf Trüp wrote: I've always been bothered by how different the two angel vessels were. Other than looks and general detachment from reality, they have absolutely nothing in common. The only explanation for their current personalities are the environments that they grew up in, right? Rei was raised by Gendo and, though he intended from the beginning to use her as a tool and was a generally aloof father figure, he was a father figure none the less, and we see both of them at their happiest around each other. Their relationship wasn't healthy, but there was clearly love between them. Kaworu, on the other hand, we no next to nothing about. He was raised in secret in some undisclosed location and that's about it. Judging from the very brief interaction we see between him and SEELE and the fact that he seems to be well aware of his identity as an angel, we can assume he was treated as even less of a person than Rei was. So why, exactly, is Kaworu so much more extroverted and friendly than Rei is? Am I overlooking something?

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Apr 04, 2013 6:52 pm Kaworu was Seele's artifical messiah. To the contrary, I imagine that Kaworu was beloved by Seele, or at least respected by them. He certainly seems rather cultured, and a classical education is something one rarely gives to someone whom they regard as less than a person. Kaworu seems to have went against Seele's plan because he knew it wasn't in the best interests of the Lilim. There seems to be a bitterness in a certain Seele lord in End of Evangelion when he says that they can and will initiate Third Impact without Adam or the Angels. He wouldn't have said this if it were their scenario all along. Kaworu betrayed them.

[]: Re: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Apr 04, 2013 9:24 pm Oenara wrote: The current scientific consensus seems to be that genes influence the personality more than environment does.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 04, 2013 9:32 pm I wouldn't say he has better socialization and personalization. He refers to humans as another species, after all. He's just more at peace with who and what he is, and more mellow. Maybe it's because his other self is just an embryo, and Rei lives over top of her gigantic dough form, constantly being called home.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Apr 04, 2013 10:56 pm Chuckman wrote: I wouldn't say he has better socialization and personalization. He refers to humans as another species, after all.

[]: Re: Rei's Upbringing Vs Kaworu's             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 05, 2013 7:19 am Güf Trüp wrote: I've always been bothered by how different the two angel vessels were. Other than looks and general detachment from reality, they have absolutely nothing in common. The only explanation for their current personalities are the environments that they grew up in, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:44 am I don't think their upbringings were that different, it's probably their attitude towards a similar situation that makes up for the difference. But that's something that I keep saying, when ppl debate how much of the way Rei acts is inherent in her nature, and how much the result of external damage by being raised in a lab and, which is probably the main point, knowing she's replaceable: Look at our other example of a SoL-in-a-human-body, who seems to quite enjoy his existance. Rei's technically more human than Kaworu, from a purely physical level, since Lillith was the progenitor of humanity/ a FoK organism. Heck, Mini!Rei acted a whole lot different, like smiling is most her appearances and actually looking up to Ritsuko when she introduced herself and mischieviously annoying people like any other little girl. It would seem that those last words of Naoko's to her engrained themselves quite deeply, comparable of the "echo" of number II that the third clone seemed to have, making her randomly burst into tears for a reason she can't understand etc. It's also the fact that a little of imput had her acting surprisingly normal (around Gendo - or simply rewatch the episodes 15 to 18) - If she was inherently that way, she may learn ways around it to handle certain issues, but she'd always need a concious effort, like an autistic person. No spontaneus blushes. In fact, Rei doesn't act half as "emotionless" as people commonly state, perhaps only a bit more than we'd expect it of a girl. Really, go through the script of episode 5 and underline every single time a reaction from her is specified. (I once saw a recently eye-opening movie where some women tried to disguise themselves as men. The first thing they were told was to stop randomly smiling all the time, unless there was a good reason. and the ladies had quite a hard time surpressing it - It was quite shocking, to see how deeply and unconciously girls are reared to be "pleasant to men". But it's totally a learned thing. That said, smiling itself is a learned thing. When a baby is born, they can get angry when they don't get what they want, the cry a lot etc, but they don't smile until they're one month old or so, copying it off the parents. I remember what a huge milestone that was when my little sister learned to do that, because before, she could only express herself to complain about negative things (ie, bawling) but then, that little baby could suddenly tell us when she liked things/give us positive feedback. Rei probably never was a Baby, she was created as a kindergartener (because a toddler with a projectable AT-Field is probably just not practicable. Every tantrum could result in an unsheduled Third Impact. And how'd you get her to take her pills? ) and may have missed some things you were supposed to learn in those first three years, like how to make others understand your feelings. She has language skills, but she was most likely "programmed" with those. Or, Rei may have simply copied a lot of her gestures etc. off Gendo, because, who else was there? And Gendo doesn't change his expression that often, either. ) From what we can guess about Kaworu, it's not much of an assumption that he's got that vocabulary of his from regularly talking to the Seele dudes, because it's the same high, philosophic talk. (...which often goes over poor Shinji's head) It's not Kaworu seemed particularly experienced - He was fairly unaware of what humans may consider akward or an intrusion into personal space, for example. I mean, if he'd walk into a school like this, he'd get called out on thatduring the first three days, and would presumably try to correct that. What kind of 15 year old would not expect anyone getting a a bit of a double meaning to "Let's got to bed together?" Regardless of his interest in Shinji, he only seems to have asked this for the sole sake of continueing their conversation, and in contrast, Shinji's like "No, er, I mean...- " So whatever humans he had contact with, it probably weren't youngsters of his age. He may have come across some compassionate scientists in whatever lab SEELE raised him in, or, he may not even need company (even though he seems to enjoy it, at least) the way we humans do, given that he's technically an "entire" being and not an individual of a "fractured" being like a Lillithian life form, he just looks like one. SEELE may have taught him one thing or so to ensure he can at least pass for human for a few days, but they're even less concerned with anyone's life or welfare than Gendo is. I mean, they were very much sending Kaworu to his death. Both him and Rei are inexperienced with human contact, it's more that he just goes ahead regardless, naively, in a way. It may help that he seems to have a solid idea of who he is, or at least there was no indication that he has any sort of identity conflict. He's not the sort of character that can really be shown from the "inside"....

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Fri Apr 05, 2013 6:54 pm I thought Rei I had more emotions because Gendo taught her emotions. That's why she acted like a normal little girl. When she died and was replaced, Gendo probably realised that if Rei grew up with normal emotions and suddenly died, her clone replacement would have the blank slate personality.That's when people would notice something's wrong, and start investigating. Rei I was easy to replace because Gendo only introduced (onscreen, anway) Rei to Naoko and Ritsuko. Naoko died and Ritsuko found out about the whole cloning thing, so there was no issue. Rei III had the same outward personality as Rei II. Just imagine what would have happened if Rei II's personality had been like, let's say, AU Genki!Rei. If her personality got reset, that would have been awkward

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Fri Apr 05, 2013 10:19 pm One thing to consider is that Kaworu is (probably) effectively older than Rei. Kaworu could have been created as early as 2000. It's possible that he was created as a baby without any accelerated growth. Rei, however, was created in 2004 at the earliest (since it happened after Yui "died", and we know Shinji was three years old when it happened). We also know that in 2010 she still looked like a child almost certainly under 9 years old (judging by appearance), indicating that her accelerated growth occurred mostly somewhere in the 2010-2015 range. So regardless of how Rei was raised, Kaworu most likely had at least a few years more as a young teenager to learn about himself and the world around him. At that age, that can make a huge difference. Rena wrote: I thought Rei I had more emotions because Gendo taught her emotions. That's why she acted like a normal little girl. When she died and was replaced, Gendo probably realised that if Rei grew up with normal emotions and suddenly died, her clone replacement would have the blank slate personality.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Apr 05, 2013 11:20 pm Paranoid wrote: Personally, I think the reason that he seemed to dote more on Rei I than Rei II (which is conjecture in and of itself; there's no hard proof of this) is because he was afraid. While he did see Rei as replaceable, we do know that he was protective of her when possible, and that he panicked when he thought she was hurt in the experiment. It's like losing Yui all over again for him. We have no knowledge of how he reacted when Rei I died, but maybe he was so crushed that he decided to avoid being as close to Rei II and distanced himself from her, leading to her stunted emotional development. It would be much easier if she were a doll, rather than a constant living reminder of his dead wife, emotions and all.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Apr 05, 2013 11:24 pm I'd like to put out the idea that Rei was traumatized by being strangled for speaking out- and then having her spirit removed and forced into another body- and so became terrified to express herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sat Apr 06, 2013 9:48 am As has been mentioned, I think Rei was so introverted, shy and quiet was because her emotional, independent, outgoing, rebellious side had been shut away in a different body, first in Rei 1 and then in Unit 00, rather than because of her upbringing.. I could copy my whole theory about Rei's soul, but for the sake of brevity I won't.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Sun Apr 07, 2013 12:42 am I see it as simple as Kaworu had the whole soul of Adam vs. Rei only had a fraction of Lilith's

[]:             Quote Post by Xiel » Sun May 12, 2013 12:39 pm I don't think Kaworu's upbringing was very social, at least if we notice the little cues we've given in episode 24: he's for the first time paying attention to a person (Shinji), he remarks how "nice" is to have a place to call his own (implying he doesn't have one place to return as home), and how Shinji made his life meaningful. Then we can bring NGE 2 to fill some holes where effectively Kaworu was isolated and only learned the warmth of people in his stay at Nerv, despite all his former attempts. That's unravelled in his Maze of Heart how he doesn't care if people would betray him as long he can care for them. He's just... the extreme opposite of Shinji. Shinji shyly hungers for love while Kaworu openly longs to provide love to fight off their loneliness. At least that's my impression. The crippling fear that is to be forever alone in the outcome the lilin were destroyed haunts him in that game. His seeking Lilith or Adam are like seeking a place he's called 'home'. He is as confused with his identity as Rei, but for different reasons (he wants to find out who are the first ancestral race, the purpose he's forgotten, where he came from, what is his future, etc). Like Kendrix stated in the beginning. It's the attitude they take completely different. Anno said all Eva characters, except for Mari, have the same problems deep down (according to Tsurumaki, at least), they are just different 'seasonings'. As Shinji said to Misato. Kaworu's a little like him and a little like Rei. Not that different at all. They have a different approach to their circumstances. Can't you say the same about Gendo? Asuka? Misato?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun May 12, 2013 9:37 pm Lavinius wrote: I'd like to put out the idea that Rei was traumatized by being strangled for speaking out- and then having her spirit removed and forced into another body- and so became terrified to express herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun May 12, 2013 10:36 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Interesting: although that would make her seem like a bit of a bitch, since her "speaking out" was a rather malicious attack at Naoko's insecurities over her relationship with Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun May 12, 2013 11:32 pm I think the primary difference between Rei and Kaworu is that Rei was not created to be articulate. Not being articulate also leads to a dearth of consciousness. It's not that Rei isn't intelligent or thinking, but that her agency is locked up in her subconscious mind while what surface awareness she does possess is occupied by the duties and worldview she grew up with. She does have a will, but it is muted and the small portion of it that has risen to her conscious mind to be articulated has been co-opted and occupied by Gendo. Such a "dearth of consciousness" may or may not be possible to be cultivated in a normal human (Rei seems to lack curiosity, which young children tend to exhibit), but apparently Rei's soul is special since it was "salvaged" from what remained of Yui. My guess is that she had potential for normal conscious development, but never needed it,a nd so skipped the "superfluous" processes entirely. In the timeframe of the show, Shinji affected her slightly, but nevertheless only on a subconscious or "this is new territory" level, such as when she smiles after killing the tetrahedron angel or when she blushes when Shinji says she looks motherly. Kaworu on the other hand is highly articulate, being respected by SEELE after all, as a godly figure and not just a vessel like Rei. His philosophical language indicates that he probably spent much of his childhood in the company of SEELE elites. His excellent linguistic command also makes him super articulate and thus also extremely conscious of the world he lives in - quite the opposite of Rei. Unfortunately he only appeared for one episode so we don't know much more about him for sure.

Article: Reis Upbringing Vs Kaworus
GE 2 to fill some holes where effectively Kaworu was isolated and only learned the warmth of people in his stay at Nerv, despite all his former attempts. That's unravelled in his Maze of Heart how he doesn't care if people would betray him as long he can care for them. He's just... the extreme opposite of Shinji. Shinji shyly hungers for love while Kaworu openly longs to provide love to fight off their loneliness. At least that's my impression. The crippling fear that is to be forever alone in the outcome the lilin were destroyed haunts him in that game. His seeking Lilith or Adam are like seeking a place he's called 'home'. He is as confused with his identity as Rei, but for different reasons (he wants to find out who are the first ancestral race, the purpose he's forgotten, where he came from, what is his future, etc). Like Kendrix stated in the beginning. It's the attitude they take completely different. Anno said all Eva characters, except for Mari, have the same problems deep down (according to Tsurumaki, at least), they are just different 'seasonings'. As Shinji said to Misato. Kaworu's a little like him and a little like Rei. Not that different at all. They have a different approach to their circumstances. Can't you say the same about Gendo? Asuka? Misato?

Not being articulate also leads to a dearth of consciousness. It's not that Rei isn't intelligent or thinking, but that her agency is locked

Article: Reis Upbringing Vs Kaworus
 pm I think the primary difference between Rei and Kaworu is that Rei was not created to be articulate. Not being articulate also leads to a dearth of consciousness. It's not that Rei isn't intelligent or thinking, but that her agency is locked up in her subconscious mind while what surface awareness she does possess is occupied by the duties and worldview she grew up with. She does have a will, but it is muted and the small portion of it that has risen to her conscious mind to be articulated has been co-opted and occupied by Gendo. Such a "dearth of consciousness" may or may not be possible to be cultivated in a normal human (Rei seems to lack curiosity, which young children tend to exhibit), but apparently Rei's soul is special since it was "salvaged" from what remained of Yui. My guess is that she had potential for normal conscious development, but never needed it,a nd so skipped the "superfluous" processes entirely. In the timeframe of the show, Shinji affected her slightly, but nevertheless only on a subconscious or "this is new territory" level, such as when she smiles after killing the tetrahedron angel or when she blushes when Shinji says she looks motherly. Kaworu on the other hand is highly articulate, being respected by SEELE after all, as a godly figure and not just a vessel like Rei. His philosophical language indicates that he probably spent much of his childhood in the company of SEELE elites. His excellent linguistic command also makes him super articulate and thus also extremely conscious of the world he lives in - quite the opposite of Rei. Unfortunately he only appeared for one episode so we don't know much more about him for sure.
